name: Mobia Medical, Inc.
business: Mobia Medical, Inc. commercializes the Vivistim System, an implantable device for chronic ischemic stroke survivors with moderate to severe upper extremity impairments. The system is sold to primary and comprehensive stroke centers in the United States. Revenue is derived entirely from sales of the Vivistim System to customers that purchase initial stocking orders and reorder replenishment product as procedures are performed.

exposures.sentence: Our Vivistim Paired Vagus Nerve Stimulation (Paired VNS) System is the first and only clinically-validated, FDA-approved solution for chronic ischemic stroke survivors with moderate to severe upper extremity impairments.

catalysts.event: CMS assigned Vivistim System implantation procedure to a New Technology Ambulatory Payment Classification (APC), establishing an elevated payment level.
catalysts.sentence: Effective January 1, 2026, this code was assigned to a New Technology Ambulatory Payment Classification (APC) by CMS, which established an elevated payment level.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 10 million stroke survivors in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on a report published by the AHA in 2025, we estimate that there are currently approximately 10 million stroke survivors in the United States, the majority of whom are living with chronic impairments.

tamClaims.claim: More than 4 million chronic ischemic stroke survivors with moderate to severe upper extremity impairment.
tamClaims.sentence: This equates to approximately 9 million ischemic stroke survivors in the United States, of which we estimate that more than 4 million are chronic ischemic stroke survivors living with moderate to severe upper extremity impairment.

tamClaims.claim: Initial market opportunity comprises approximately 1 million survivors, representing over $30 billion based on average selling price.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that an initial market opportunity comprises approximately 1 million of those survivors that demonstrate the requisite overall health, cognition and motivation to participate in therapy and have received some amount of post-stroke therapy, which we estimate represents an initial market opportunity of over $30 billion based on the average selling price of the Vivistim System.

tamClaims.claim: Each year approximately 200,000 new stroke survivors meet the indication, with 50,000 representative of initial market opportunity.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on a report published by the AHA in 2025, we estimate that each year approximately 200,000 new stroke survivors in the United States meet our indication for use, with 50,000 of these survivors representative of our initial market opportunity.
